plain_definition: A trading venue that brings together multiple buyers' and sellers' orders for virtual assets under fixed, non-discretionary rules -- broadly, an exchange or order-matching venue, as distinct from a firm that deals in virtual assets as principal on its own book. 'Operating a Multilateral Trading Facility' is one of the eight virtual-asset activities the UAE's Capital Market Authority (CMA) licenses under its Virtual Assets Framework.
formal_definition: Named as one of the eight regulated virtual-asset activities in the CMA's Virtual Assets Framework, announced 13 April 2026 (see the cma glossary entry): 'Operating a Multilateral Trading Facility'. This project's own live fetch of the CMA's announcement page returned only the headline text confirming the activity's name and the framework's five-module, eight-activity structure; the fuller statutory definition of the term as used in the Framework itself could not be retrieved (the CMA's news article did not render beyond its headline/listing content on direct fetch) and is not asserted here.
